title: Blackened Cod & Chickpea Smacked Cucumber Salad
summary: A punchy, protein-packed salad topped with charred miso-glazed cod. Full of crunch, zing & savoury flavour. This one's going to become a regular fixture on the dinner table.

method.description: Mix the white miso and honey together in a small bowl, then rub it all over the cod fillets. Leave to marinate in the fridge for at least 30 mins — ideally up to 5 hours.

Trim the cucumber and cut it into quarters, widthways. Use a rolling pin or something heavy to smack the cucumber until it splits, then chop into rough 2cm chunks. Thinly slice the celery. Pick the coriander leaves and thinly slice the stems.
Once the cod has marinated, remove it from the fridge and let it come to room temperature. Heat the grill to high.

Line a baking tray with foil, place the cod fillets on top and slide under the grill for 6–10 mins until cooked through and starting to char. If the marinade is catching too quickly, move the tray down a shelf or lower the grill temperature slightly.
While the cod is grilling, stir all the dressing ingredients together in a bowl.
Once the fish is cooked, toss the chickpeas, cucumber, celery, coriander and quinoa into the dressing and mix well. Share the salad between plates and top with the blackened cod.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
